source.title: Effective context engineering for AI agents

viewerQuestion: Why do AI Agents lose focus even when their prompts and histories keep getting longer?
centralThesis: AI Agent reliability comes from continuously curating a finite attention budget: keep the smallest sufficient set of high-signal instructions, tools, examples, retrieved evidence, and working state instead of preloading everything.
reusableArtifact: Minimum High-Signal Context Pack: Outcome, Operating Rules, Tool Map, Canonical Examples, Retrieval Index, Working State

P0: Context is a finite attention budget rather than an unlimited knowledge container
More tokens can dilute decisive signals instead of improving behavior
The six-part context pack is the reusable operating method
P1: Set system instructions at the right altitude
Keep tool contracts clear, efficient, and minimally overlapping
Use a small set of canonical examples
Retrieve detailed context just in time through lightweight references
Preserve long-task coherence through compaction, notes, or specialized agents
P2: Anthropic's distinction between prompt engineering and iterative context engineering
The trade-off between preloading speed and just-in-time focus
Claude Code examples of lightweight references, shell exploration, and persistent notes
Task-characteristic boundaries for compaction, notes, and sub-agent architectures
P3Removed: Product calls to action and platform setup details
Repeated definitions that add no mechanism or action
Unsupported claims that one context strategy is universally best

chapters.label: Introduction
chapters.promise: See why a longer prompt can still make an AI Agent forget the point

chapters.label: Attention Budget
chapters.promise: Treat context as scarce attention instead of unlimited storage

chapters.label: High-Signal Inputs
chapters.promise: Choose instructions, tools, and examples that guide without crowding

chapters.label: Retrieve on Demand
chapters.promise: Load detail when it becomes relevant instead of preloading everything

chapters.label: Long-Horizon Memory
chapters.promise: Preserve decisions and progress across long tasks and context resets

chapters.label: Context Pack
chapters.promise: Build one reusable six-part context package for real AI Agent work

chapters.label: Summary
chapters.promise: Keep the signal, retrieve the detail, and preserve working state

visualSemantics.humanRole: defines outcomes, constraints, retrieval boundaries, and acceptance criteria
visualSemantics.agentRole: selects high-signal context, retrieves evidence, updates state, and continues the task
visualSemantics.artifact: one compact context packet that replaces a swelling pile of prompts and history

factBoundaries: Minimal means sufficient and high-signal, not automatically short
Context rot is a reliability warning, not proof that every longer context performs worse on every task
Just-in-time retrieval is slower than preloading and depends on good tools and heuristics
Compaction can remove subtle information whose importance appears later
Sub-agents help when focused parallel exploration justifies coordination cost

contentEdit.keptEvidence: All P0 and P1 material plus P2 evidence that establishes source credibility, mechanism, transfer, or a boundary
contentEdit.removedOnly: Repeated wording, product promotion, acknowledgements, and details that add no new fact, mechanism, boundary, step, or understanding value
